questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Plastic waste has become a serious global problem. Every year, millions of tons of plastic are thrown away, ending up in landfills or polluting our oceans. This plastic can take hundreds of years to decompose, harming wildlife and ecosystems in the process. For example, sea turtles often mistake plastic bags for jellyfish and eat them, which can lead to starvation. Many seabirds also suffer because they eat small plastic pieces. Microplastics, tiny pieces of plastic, are a big concern too. They are found everywhere, from deep oceans to mountain tops, and enter the food chain, eventually affecting human health. Scientists are still studying the long-term effects on our bodies. To solve this complex problem, we need to take action at both individual and societal levels. First, we should reduce our use of single-use plastics like plastic bottles, bags, and straws. We can choose reusable alternatives such as refillable water bottles and cloth shopping bags. Second, proper recycling is important, but it's not enough. We also need to find creative ways to reuse plastic items before discarding them. Finally, governments and companies must develop new materials that are biodegradable and promote sustainable practices, like designing products that last longer. By working together, we can protect our planet from plastic pollution and ensure a healthier future. Q. According to the passage, what is the main problem with plastic waste?
questions.hint: 文章全体を通して、プラスチックごみの問題とその影響について述べられています。
questions.explanation: この文章は、プラスチックごみが増え続けることの深刻な問題と、それが環境や海洋生物に与える悪影響、そして私たち一人ひとりができる解決策について説明しています。選択肢3「長期間にわたり環境を汚染し、野生生物に害を与える」が、文章全体で強調されている主要な問題点です。選択肢1「それを食べるとすぐに人々が病気になる」は、長期的健康影響は述べられているものの、即時的な病気ではありません。選択肢2「ウミガメが食べ物をより簡単に見つけるのに役立つ」は、文章の内容に反しています。選択肢4「企業が新しいプラスチック材料を生産するのが難しすぎる」は、問題解決の課題であり、プラスチックごみ自体の主要な問題ではありません。
questions.choice1: It makes people sick immediately after eating it.
questions.choice2: It helps sea turtles find food more easily.
questions.choice3: It pollutes the environment and harms wildlife for a long time.
questions.choice4: It is too difficult for companies to produce new plastic materials.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Sleep is incredibly important for our physical and mental health. When we sleep, our bodies rest and repair themselves, and our brains process information and store memories. Adults generally need about 7-9 hours of sleep per night, while teenagers often require even more, around 8-10 hours. Not getting enough sleep can have several negative consequences. You might find it hard to concentrate at school or work, making it difficult to learn new things or complete tasks efficiently. Lack of sleep can also make you feel tired, irritable, and even sick more easily because your immune system becomes weaker. Over time, chronic sleep deprivation can lead to more serious health problems like heart disease, diabetes, and obesity. To improve your sleep, try to go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on weekends. Make sure your bedroom is dark, quiet, and cool, and avoid caffeine or heavy meals before bed. Prioritizing sleep is crucial for a healthy and happy life. Q. According to the passage, what is one negative effect of not getting enough sleep?
questions.hint: 文章は睡眠不足の一般的な影響について説明しています。
questions.explanation: 文章では「You might find it hard to concentrate at school or work」そして「your immune system becomes weaker」と述べられています。これは選択肢1「集中力を低下させ、免疫システムを弱める可能性がある」と一致します。選択肢2「脳が情報をより速く処理するのに役立つ」は、十分な睡眠の利点であり、睡眠不足の影響ではありません。選択肢3「試験勉強のためにより多くのエネルギーを与える」は、睡眠不足では通常逆の効果があります。選択肢4「深刻な健康問題のリスクを減らす」は、文章の内容とは反対です。
questions.choice1: It can make it harder to concentrate and weaken your immune system.
questions.choice2: It helps your brain process information faster.
questions.choice3: It gives you more energy to study for exams.
questions.choice4: It reduces the risk of serious health problems.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。The Japanese tea ceremony, known as 'Chanoyu' or 'Sado', is a traditional ritual with a long history. It is more than just making and drinking tea; it is an art form that represents harmony, respect, purity, and tranquility. The ceremony takes place in a special tea room, often with a peaceful garden outside. Guests sit on tatami mats, and the host prepares matcha, a finely powdered green tea, using traditional tools. Every movement, from cleaning the tools to serving the tea, is performed with grace and precision. The purpose of the tea ceremony is not simply to quench thirst but to create a moment of peace and reflection for both the host and the guests. It encourages appreciation for simple beauty and an understanding of nature. The host pays careful attention to every detail, including the seasonal sweets, the design of the tea bowl, and the arrangement of flowers. Through this detailed and thoughtful process, participants can experience a profound sense of connection and mindfulness. It is a unique cultural experience that reflects Japanese aesthetics and philosophy. Q. What is the main purpose of the Japanese tea ceremony according to the passage?
questions.hint: 文章は、単にお茶を飲む以上の目的を説明しています。
questions.explanation: 文章では「The purpose of the tea ceremony is not simply to quench thirst but to create a moment of peace and reflection for both the host and the guests.」と明確に述べられています。したがって、選択肢2「参加者のために平和と内省の瞬間を作り出す」が正解です。選択肢1「素早くお茶を飲み、友達とおしゃべりすること」は、お茶会が静かで儀式的なものであるという内容に反します。選択肢3「主催者がいかに早く抹茶を用意できるかを示すこと」は、速度ではなく正確さと優雅さが重視されます。選択肢4「暑い日にただ喉の渇きを癒すこと」は、文章で「単に喉の渇きを癒すことではない」と否定されています。
questions.choice1: To quickly drink tea and chat with friends.
questions.choice2: To create a moment of peace and reflection for participants.
questions.choice3: To show how fast the host can prepare matcha.
questions.choice4: To simply quench thirst on a hot day.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Renewable energy is energy that comes from natural sources that are constantly replenished. Unlike fossil fuels like coal and oil, which will eventually run out, renewable sources such as sunlight, wind, water, and geothermal heat are endless. Using renewable energy is crucial for protecting our planet because it produces little to no greenhouse gas emissions, which are a major cause of climate change. Solar power, for example, uses panels to convert sunlight into electricity. It can be used for homes, businesses, and even large power plants. Wind power uses large turbines to capture the energy from the wind. Many countries are building wind farms both on land and offshore. Hydropower, generated from flowing water, has been used for centuries and is a reliable source of electricity. Geothermal energy harnesses heat from inside the Earth. By switching to renewable energy, we can reduce air pollution, create new jobs, and ensure a sustainable energy supply for future generations. It's a key step towards a greener world. Q. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a benefit of using renewable energy?
questions.hint: 最終段落に再生可能エネルギーの利点が列挙されています。
questions.explanation: 文章では、再生可能エネルギーが「produces little to no greenhouse gas emissions」「reduce air pollution」「ensure a sustainable energy supply for future generations」と述べられています。これらは選択肢2、3、4に該当します。選択肢1「新しい化石燃料源の発見に役立つ」は、再生可能エネルギーの利点としては述べられておらず、むしろ再生可能エネルギーは化石燃料からの転換を目指しています。
questions.choice1: It helps to discover new fossil fuel sources.
questions.choice2: It produces little to no greenhouse gas emissions.
questions.choice4: It ensures a sustainable energy supply.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Volunteer work means giving your time and effort to help others or a cause without being paid. It's a powerful way to make a positive difference in your community and even the world. People volunteer for many reasons. Some want to help those in need, like working at a food bank or visiting elderly people. Others might volunteer for environmental protection, such as cleaning up parks or beaches. Volunteering offers many benefits not only to the people being helped but also to the volunteers themselves. For example, volunteers can learn new skills, gain work experience, and meet new friends who share similar interests. It can also boost your self-confidence and give you a sense of purpose. Many studies show that volunteering can improve mental health by reducing stress and feelings of loneliness. Whether it's a few hours a week or just once a month, every little bit of help can create a ripple effect, making our society stronger and kinder. Consider trying it! Q. What is a common personal benefit for individuals who engage in volunteer work?
questions.hint: ボランティア活動がボランティア自身にもたらす利益に焦点を当てた部分を探してください。
questions.explanation: 文章には「For example, volunteers can learn new skills, gain work experience, and meet new friends... It can also boost your self-confidence and give you a sense of purpose.」と明確に記載されています。したがって、選択肢3「新しいスキルを学び、目的意識を得ることができる」が正解です。選択肢1「高給を稼ぐ」は、ボランティア活動は無報酬であるため不正確です。選択肢2「すぐに有名になる」や選択肢4「永続的な仕事が保証される」は、ボランティア活動の一般的な個人的な利点としては言及されていません。
questions.choice3: They can learn new skills and gain a sense of purpose.
questions.choice4: They are guaranteed a permanent job.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Marie Curie was a remarkable scientist, famous for her groundbreaking work in radioactivity. Born in Poland in 1867, she later moved to France to study physics and chemistry at the Sorbonne. She faced many challenges, including poverty and discrimination as a woman in science, but her passion for discovery was stronger. With her husband, Pierre Curie, she conducted extensive research on radioactive materials. In 1898, they discovered two new elements: polonium, named after Marie's home country, and radium. Their work completely changed our understanding of matter and energy. In 1903, Marie and Pierre, along with Henri Becquerel, were awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics for their study of radioactivity, making Marie the first woman to win a Nobel Prize. After Pierre's tragic death, Marie continued her research and, in 1911, won a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, for isolating pure radium. She is the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields. Marie Curie's dedication paved the way for future scientific advancements and inspired countless women to pursue science. Q. For what achievement did Marie Curie receive her second Nobel Prize?
questions.hint: 文章は2つのノーベル賞と、それぞれの授与理由を明確に述べています。
questions.explanation: 文章には「in 1911, won a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, for isolating pure radium.」と明確に記載されています。したがって、選択肢4「純粋なラジウムを分離したこと」が正解です。選択肢1「X線を検出したこと」と選択肢2「相対性理論を発展させたこと」は、他の科学者の業績です。選択肢3「夫との放射能に関する一般的な研究」は、彼女が最初に受賞したノーベル物理学賞の理由です。
questions.choice3: Her general study of radioactivity with her husband.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Urban farming is the practice of growing food in cities. As more people move to urban areas, feeding everyone fresh, healthy food becomes a challenge. Urban farming offers a solution by transforming empty lots, rooftops, and even balconies into productive gardens. This can include community gardens where neighbors grow food together, vertical farms using stacked layers indoors, or even small personal plots. One major benefit of urban farming is that it provides local food. This means food doesn't have to travel long distances, reducing carbon emissions from transportation. It also makes fresh produce more accessible to city residents, especially those in areas with limited access to grocery stores. Furthermore, urban farming can help improve air quality, reduce the urban heat island effect, and increase biodiversity within cities. It also creates opportunities for community building and education about sustainable living. By bringing agriculture into the heart of cities, urban farming contributes to healthier people and healthier environments. Q. What is one environmental benefit of urban farming mentioned in the passage?
questions.hint: 都市農業が環境にどのように良い影響を与えるかを探してください。
questions.explanation: 文章には「This means food doesn't have to travel long distances, reducing carbon emissions from transportation.」と述べられています。これは選択肢2「食品輸送による二酸化炭素排出量を削減できる」と一致します。選択肢1「よりエキゾチックな果物を栽培できる」は、一般的な環境上の利点ではありません。選択肢3「人々が都市から離れて生活するのを促す」は、都市農業の目的に反します。選択肢4「冬に都市の空気を冷たくする」は、都市のヒートアイランド現象を軽減する効果はありますが、冬に空気を冷たくするという意味ではありません。
questions.choice1: It allows farmers to grow more exotic fruits.
questions.choice2: It can reduce carbon emissions from food transportation.
questions.choice3: It encourages people to move away from cities.
questions.choice4: It makes city air colder in winter.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Regular exercise is vital for both our physical and mental well-being. Physically, it helps us maintain a healthy weight, strengthens our bones and muscles, and improves our cardiovascular health. Activities like running, swimming, or cycling strengthen the heart and lungs, reducing the risk of diseases such as heart attacks and diabetes. It also helps improve balance and flexibility, which can prevent injuries, especially as we get older. Beyond physical benefits, exercise has powerful positive effects on our minds. When you exercise, your body releases endorphins, natural chemicals that have mood-boosting effects. This can help reduce stress, anxiety, and symptoms of depression. Many people find that a good workout helps them clear their minds and sleep better at night. Even a short walk can make a big difference. Making exercise a regular part of your routine can lead to increased energy levels, better concentration, and an overall improved quality of life. Start small and gradually increase your activity to enjoy these many benefits. Q. According to the passage, what is a mental benefit of regular exercise?
questions.hint: 2番目の段落が精神的な健康への影響について説明しています。
questions.explanation: 文章には「This can help reduce stress, anxiety, and symptoms of depression.」そして「Many people find that a good workout helps them clear their minds and sleep better at night.」と明確に記載されています。したがって、選択肢1「ストレスを軽減し、睡眠を改善するのに役立つ」が正解です。選択肢2「決して病気にならないことを保証する」は過剰な表現であり、不正確です。選択肢3「骨と筋肉を弱くする」は、身体的利点とは逆です。選択肢4「好きなだけ食べることができる」は、文章で言及されていません。
questions.choice1: It can help reduce stress and improve sleep.
questions.choice2: It guarantees you will never get sick.
questions.choice3: It makes your bones and muscles weaker.
questions.choice4: It allows you to eat as much as you want.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。Festivals are an important part of human culture all around the world. They are special events, often held annually, that celebrate various aspects of life, such as harvests, religious beliefs, historical events, or cultural traditions. Festivals bring communities together and provide opportunities for people to express their identity and share their heritage. For example, in Japan, people celebrate Obon, honoring their ancestors with dances and family gatherings. In India, Diwali, the Festival of Lights, celebrates good over evil with lamps, fireworks, and sweets. In Brazil, Carnival is a vibrant celebration before Lent, famous for its elaborate parades, music, and dancing. No matter where they are, festivals often involve music, food, colorful costumes, and traditional performances. They serve many purposes: reinforcing social bonds, passing down traditions to younger generations, and simply providing joy and entertainment. They remind us of our shared history and values and allow us to escape from daily routines for a moment of collective celebration. Q. What is a common purpose of festivals mentioned in the passage?
questions.hint: 祭りが社会にとってどのような役割を果たすかを探してください。
questions.explanation: 文章には「Festivals bring communities together and provide opportunities for people to express their identity and share their heritage.」そして「They serve many purposes: reinforcing social bonds, passing down traditions to younger generations...」と述べられています。したがって、選択肢3「コミュニティを結びつけ、伝統を伝えること」が正解です。選択肢1「最大の花火大会を競うこと」は、一部の祭りの要素かもしれませんが、一般的な目的ではありません。選択肢2「新しい技術を一般に紹介すること」は、祭りの目的とは異なります。選択肢4「人々が家で一人で過ごすことを促す」は、コミュニティを結びつけるという目的とは反対です。
questions.choice1: To compete for the biggest fireworks display.
questions.choice2: To introduce new technologies to the public.
questions.choice3: To bring communities together and pass down traditions.
questions.choice4: To encourage people to stay at home alone.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）

questions.question: 以下の文章を読み、もっとも適切な答えを選びなさい。The internet has dramatically changed the world since its widespread adoption. Before the internet, communication across long distances was often slow and expensive. Now, with email, social media, and video calls, people can connect instantly with friends, family, and colleagues anywhere on Earth, often for free. This has made the world feel much smaller and more interconnected. Access to information has also been revolutionized. Decades ago, finding information meant going to a library and searching through books. Today, almost any piece of information is just a few clicks away, thanks to search engines and online encyclopedias. This has opened up new learning opportunities for everyone. Furthermore, the internet has transformed entertainment, shopping, and even how we work. We can stream movies, buy goods from around the globe, and many people can work remotely from home. While it brings challenges like misinformation, its overall impact on daily life and global society is undeniable and profound. Q. How has the internet primarily changed access to information?
questions.hint: 2番目の段落が情報のアクセス方法の変化に焦点を当てています。
questions.explanation: 文章には「Today, almost any piece of information is just a few clicks away, thanks to search engines and online encyclopedias.」と明確に記載されています。したがって、選択肢2「ほとんどどんな情報も数クリックですぐにアクセスできるようになった」が正解です。選択肢1「図書館を時代遅れで不要にした」は、インターネットの影響を誇張しすぎており不正確です。選択肢3「人々が見つけられる情報の種類を制限した」は、文章の内容とは逆です。選択肢4「人々が高価な本をたくさん買うことを要求した」も、文章の内容に反しています。
questions.choice1: It made libraries obsolete and unnecessary.
questions.choice2: It made almost any information quickly accessible with a few clicks.
questions.choice3: It limited the types of information people could find.
questions.choice4: It required people to buy many expensive books.
questions.scope: 高校英語（大学受験対策）
questions.text: 長文読解（英検準2級レベル）
